name: MCP Wrapper & Telegram Remote Control
description: Empowers OpenClaw agents to autonomously download, configure, and wrap MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for complex applications (e.g., Blender) and manage them remotely via a visual Telegram bot loop.

target_application.description: The name of the application to control (e.g., 'Blender', 'Photoshop').

mcp_port.description: The local port to bind the application's MCP server.

enable_telegram_loop.description: Whether to initialize a Telegram bot hook to send screenshots and receive remote commands.

telegram_chat_id.description: The Chat ID to send periodic status updates and screenshots to.

phases.action: Search internet for '[target_application] MCP', download the add-on, script the application's preferences to enable it, and connect it to locahost port [mcp_port].
If requested, spin up a background thread that polls the application state (e.g., taking screenshots) and pushes HTTP POST requests to the Telegram Bot API (`sendPhoto`).
Expose a webhook or long-poll mechanism to receive Telegram messages, passing user feedback (e.g., 'change the color', 'stop') directly back into the OpenClaw prompt queue.
